pr.title: Label assert and HSD_Panic due to how commonly they're used
pr.author: PsiLupan

summary: PR 65 was a very small merged change whose stated purpose was to label the commonly used debug/error routines assert and HSD_Panic. The available dump contains no diff, changed files, PR body, or review comments, so the record is limited to the title-level evidence.
what_changed: Labeled assert, according to the PR title.
Labeled HSD_Panic, according to the PR title.
The motivation given in the title was that these routines are commonly used, making stable labels useful across the decompilation.
smart_moves: Prioritized labels for high-frequency utility/debug functions instead of leaving repeated callsites anonymous.
Chose recognizable canonical names, assert and HSD_Panic, which would make future diff reading and callsite identification easier.
decomp_lessons: Common runtime/debug helpers are worth naming early because they appear in many unrelated files and improve readability across the project.
Even when a change is small, labeling high fan-out functions can reduce repeated reverse-engineering effort in later matching work.
Because this PR slice has no diff or files, future agents should verify exact symbol addresses and affected artifacts from repository history before relying on this record for implementation details.
naming_conventions: Use the plain name assert for the assert helper when evidence supports that symbol.
Use the HSD_ prefix for HAL/SysDolphin functions such as HSD_Panic.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Identify common panic/assert callsites and assign stable labels so subsequent function work can refer to them symbolically.
For frequently called library/debug routines, labeling the callee can be a low-risk way to improve many disassemblies at once.
follow_up_queries: Find the actual commit or repository diff for PR 65 to confirm which files or symbol maps were edited.
Search current labels for assert and HSD_Panic to verify final addresses and naming.
Inspect callsites of HSD_Panic and assert for later PRs that depended on these labels.
